spec_match.rationale: Some interactivity is present but the script payload is light and no clear API surface is invoked, so the page only loosely matches the spec's promise.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (0 buttons / 2 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (contenteditable, input, line, single).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 26 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Wire the controls to a real (or feature-detected) API call
openQuestions.detail: Beef up the script so the demo invokes the API and renders the actual return value or side effect, rather than driving purely visual state.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("When a user interacts (editing or caret navigation) with text which has ‘text-overflow: ellipsis’ set, the text switches temporarily from ellipsis to clip allowing the user to see and interact with the hidden overflow content. This feature ") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://www.w3.org/TR/css-overflow-3/#ellipsis-interaction to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~0 chars of script across 0 button(s) and 2 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
